behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error (parser output passed into hook-input handling) that can mis-handle escaped markdown-like content even when JSON is valid. Required setup: wire real parser + hook input handling modules with valid fixtures containing code fences, backticks, and `$vars`. Rejected System because behavior text is about "Valid JSON payloads ... parse successfully" and does not require real OS/network/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test that feeds multiple valid JSON payloads whose string fields include markdown-heavy content through the actual parser and hook-input handling pipeline; assert parse success, no parse-failure trace, and expected downstream handling state.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: parser-to-tracing integration error (wrong error classification/context propagation), not just raw parse logic. Required setup: malformed payloads through parser plus real trace-record construction/sink adapter in-process. Rejected System because behavior text only requires a trace record on malformed JSON, not real process I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Inject malformed JSON fixtures into the in-process parser+trace path; assert `json_parse_failed` is emitted and includes diagnostic context (e.g., error position/snippet metadata) sufficient to locate the malformed section.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: logic error in local error-policy mapping (exception/result/telemetry intent). Required setup: call the error-policy function/object directly with a parse-failure input and inspect returned handled result + telemetry intent marker. Rejected Integration because behavior text says "local error-policy path," which disqualifies needing multi-module wiring as minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the error-policy mapper with a representative parse-failure object; assert no throw, handled-result enum/status is returned, and telemetry-intent flag/field is set.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error involving real stdin stream handling, process lifecycle, and exit behavior. Required setup: spawn real hook subprocess, write malformed stdin, observe exit and emitted trace signal. Integration is insufficient because mocking subprocess/stdin can hide crash/ordering behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML output or multi-agent sequencing is involved in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: System test launches the hook executable as a subprocess, sends malformed stdin bytes, waits for completion, and asserts: process does not crash unexpectedly, expected trace signal is emitted before exit, and exit semantics match policy.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: end-to-end environment/wiring error across real invocation boundaries (stdin transport, parser execution, trace sink artifact emission). Required setup: real invocation path with subprocess and real sink artifact observation. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires "real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)".
behaviors.test_description: Run the real hook entrypoint with malformed stdin and configured trace sink target; assert the expected trace artifact is produced with correct failure type and arrives via the real sink mechanism.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: classification/regression boundary error across parser + failure-reporting path, where valid markdown content is mis-attributed as parse failure. Required setup: paired fixtures (valid markdown-heavy JSON vs malformed JSON) through shared in-process pipeline and assertion on divergent outcomes. Rejected System because behavior text focuses on distinction logic, not real OS/process effects.
behaviors.test_description: In one integration suite, run both fixture classes through parser+error/trace pipeline: valid markdown-heavy JSON must follow success path; malformed JSON must produce parse-failure path. Assert no cross-labeling or false attribution to markdown content.
